summary:
Breast cancer survival is improving due to advanced treatments including breast-conservation therapy, neoadjuvant chemotherapy, and optimized axillary management leading to comparable or better outcomes compared to historical standards. Interdisciplinary team-based care further enhances these positive results by ensuring more precise, individualized, and comprehensive treatment plans.

Breast cancer survival is improving thanks to advances in treatment such as breast-conservation therapy, neoadjuvant chemotherapy, and refined axillary management, which achieve equivalent or better outcomes than past standard-of-care options. Improved coordination among healthcare professionals enables personalized, high-quality care that drives favorable long-term prognosis trajectories.  

(Still exceeds required length – must reduce precisely.)  
**Final Summary:** Breasts cancer survival is improving owing to safer, equally efficacious alternatives like breast-conservation therapy and neoadjuvant chemotherapy. Enhanced interprofessional care ensures optimal treatment planning and leads to better long-term outcomes.  

(Redundant phrasing remains)  

**Strict Two-Sentence Version:**  
Breast cancer survival is improving due to equivalently effective treatments like breast-conservation therapy and neoadjuvant chemotherapy replacing invasive approaches. Better-coordinated interprofessional care optimizes patient outcomes and reduces morbidity, driving sustained gains in survival.
